title: Formula on dates columns

posts.author: JS_CIC (Jo Spencer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All,
New to Monday.com.
Looking to create a formula on two columns “Visit 1” + “Visit 2” to determine the last visit. If Visit 2 is blank the formula should show Visit 1 date. If both cells are blank it would return “no date set”.
There doesn’t seem to be an IFBLANK function (as in Excel) in Monday.com. Any ideas?
Many thanks,
Jo

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: I think you should ask if visit2 = “”

posts.author: JS_CIC (Jo Spencer)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @hlopezvc. I now have this working.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @JS_CIC! Welcome to the community

Happy to hear you got the formula working - would you mind sending it over? Would love to take a look!

Best,
Brett

posts.author: JS_CIC (Jo Spencer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Brett,
Here’s the formula I’m using. Monday.com returns a blank when it finds empty cells for both queries, ie no date has been assigned for visit 1 or 2:
FORMAT_DATE(IF({Date: Extn Attempt 2}=“”,{Date: Extn attempt 1},{Date: Extn Attempt 2}),“DD-MMM-YYYY, ddd”)

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml: Amazing! Thank you so much for sending that over!
